main.h(3) Library Functions Manual main.h(3)

NAME

main.h - ascii-chat Discovery Mode Entry Point Header

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int discovery_main (void)
Discovery mode entry point for unified binary.

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Discovery Mode Entry Point Header

This header exposes the discovery mode entry point for the unified binary architecture. The unified binary dispatches to discovery_main() when invoked as ascii-chat discovery.

Discovery Mode Philosophy

"ascii-chat should be as simple as making a phone call."

Discovery mode enables zero-configuration video chat:

  • One command to start: ascii-chat swift-river-mountain
  • Automatic host negotiation: Best NAT quality participant becomes host
  • Dynamic role switching: Participants can become hosts mid-session
  • Seamless failover: If host disconnects, another participant takes over

Mode Entry Point Contract

Each mode entry point (server_main, client_main, discovery_main) must:

  • Accept no arguments: int mode_main(void)
  • Options are already parsed by main dispatcher (available via GET_OPTION())
  • Return 0 on success, non-zero error code on failure
  • Perform mode-specific initialization and main loop
  • Perform complete cleanup before returning

Implementation Notes

Discovery mode combines client and server capabilities:

  • Uses session library (lib/session/) for capture, display, hosting
  • Connects to ACDS for session discovery and NAT quality assessment
  • Can transition between participant and host roles dynamically

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Version

1.0

Definition in file discovery/main.h.

Function Documentation

int discovery_main (void )

Discovery mode entry point for unified binary. This function implements the complete discovery mode lifecycle:

  • Session discovery via ACDS or LAN mDNS
  • NAT quality assessment for host selection
  • Dynamic role switching (participant <-> host)
  • Media streaming (video/audio capture and display)
  • Graceful shutdown and cleanup

Options are already parsed by the main dispatcher before this function is called, so they are available via GET_OPTION().

Returns

0 on success, non-zero error code on failure

Example

# Join a session (session string as positional argument):
ascii-chat discovery swift-river-mountain
# Or more concisely (session strings are auto-detected):
ascii-chat swift-river-mountain

Discovery mode entry point for unified binary.

Handles session discovery, host negotiation, and media flow.

Returns

0 on success, non-zero error code on failure

Definition at line 352 of file discovery/main.c.

352                          {
353   log_debug("discovery_main() starting");
354 
355   // Discovery-specific setup: initialize ACDS connection and session negotiation
356   // Note: Shared setup (keepawake, splash, terminal, capture, display, audio)
357   // is handled by session_client_like_run()
358 
359   // Get session string from options (command-line argument)
360   const char *session_string = GET_OPTION(session_string);
361   bool is_initiator = (session_string == NULL || session_string[0] == '\0');
362 
363   int port_int = GET_OPTION(port);
364 
365   log_debug("Discovery: is_initiator=%d, port=%d", is_initiator, port_int);
366 
367   // Validate mutually exclusive options: discovery_service_url vs discovery_port
368   const char *service_url = GET_OPTION(discovery_service_url);
369   if (service_url && service_url[0] != '\0') {
370     int discovery_port = GET_OPTION(discovery_port);
371     if (discovery_port != ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT) {
372       log_fatal("--discovery-service-url and --discovery-service-port are mutually exclusive");
373       return ERROR_USAGE;
374     }
375   }
376 
377   // Create discovery session configuration
378   discovery_config_t discovery_config = {
379       .acds_address = GET_OPTION(discovery_server),
380       .acds_port = (uint16_t)GET_OPTION(discovery_port),
381       .acds_url = service_url,
382       .session_string = is_initiator ? NULL : session_string,
383       .local_port = (uint16_t)port_int,
384       .on_state_change = on_discovery_state_change,
385       .on_session_ready = on_session_ready,
386       .on_error = on_discovery_error,
387       .callback_user_data = NULL,
388       .should_exit_callback = discovery_capture_should_exit_adapter,
389       .exit_callback_data = NULL,
390   };
391 
392   // Create discovery session (stored in global for discovery_run() to access)
393   g_discovery = discovery_session_create(&discovery_config);
394   if (!g_discovery) {
395     log_fatal("Failed to create discovery session");
396     return ERROR_MEMORY;
397   }
398 
399   // Start discovery session (connects to ACDS, creates/joins, initiates NAT negotiation)
400   log_debug("Discovery: starting discovery session");
401   asciichat_error_t result = discovery_session_start(g_discovery);
402   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
403     log_fatal("Failed to start discovery session: %d", result);
404     discovery_session_destroy(g_discovery);
405     g_discovery = NULL;
406     return result;
407   }
408 
409   // No network interrupt callback needed - discovery session handles its own shutdown
410   set_interrupt_callback(NULL);
411 
412   /* ========================================================================
413    * Configure and Run Shared Client-Like Session Framework
414    * ========================================================================
415    * session_client_like_run() handles all shared initialization:
416    * - Terminal output management
417    * - Keepawake system
418    * - Splash screen lifecycle
419    * - Media source selection
420    * - FPS probing
421    * - Audio initialization
422    * - Display context creation
423    * - Proper cleanup ordering
424    *
425    * Discovery mode provides:
426    * - discovery_run() callback: NAT negotiation, role determination, media handling
427    * - discovery_keyboard_handler: interactive controls for participant role
428    */
429 
430   session_client_like_config_t config = {
431       .run_fn = discovery_run,
432       .run_user_data = NULL,
433       .tcp_client = NULL,
434       .websocket_client = NULL,
435       .discovery = (void *)g_discovery, // Opaque pointer to discovery session
436       .custom_should_exit = NULL,
437       .exit_user_data = NULL,
438       .keyboard_handler = discovery_keyboard_handler,
439       .max_reconnect_attempts = 0, // Discovery doesn't retry - role is determined once
440       .should_reconnect_callback = NULL,
441       .reconnect_user_data = NULL,
442       .reconnect_delay_ms = 0,
443       .print_newline_on_tty_exit = false, // Server/participant manages cursor
444   };
445 
446   log_debug("Discovery: calling session_client_like_run()");
447   asciichat_error_t session_result = session_client_like_run(&config);
448   log_debug("Discovery: session_client_like_run() returned %d", session_result);
449 
450   // Cleanup discovery session
451   log_debug("Discovery: cleaning up");
452 
453   if (g_discovery) {
454     discovery_session_stop(g_discovery);
455     discovery_session_destroy(g_discovery);
456     g_discovery = NULL;
457   }
458 
459   return (session_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) ? 0 : (int)session_result;
460 }




References ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, discovery_session_create(),
    discovery_session_destroy(), discovery_session_start(),
    discovery_session_stop(), ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_USAGE,
    GET_OPTION, log_debug, log_fatal,
    session_client_like_run(), and set_interrupt_callback().
